questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead

The right lane ends ahead; merge left

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down

Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light changes
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light means you must come to a full stop and stay stopped until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: An eight-sided (octagonal) red sign always means what?
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
No passing
questions.explanation: The octagonal shape and red color are used only for stop signs, so you can recognize them even when partly covered.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely, then go
The light is broken; ignore it
Stop and wait for a green light
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic light require you to do?
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe, as at a stop sign
Drive through without slowing
Stop and wait for the light to turn green
Yield only to pedestrians
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop fully, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A diamond-shaped traffic sign is used to do what?
choices: Warn of hazards or changing conditions ahead
Mark a stop or yield point
Show speed limits
Give route or destination information
questions.explanation: Diamond-shaped signs are warning signs that alert drivers to conditions such as curves, merges, or pedestrian crossings ahead.

questions.prompt: What color are signs that warn of construction and maintenance work zones?
choices: Orange
Blue
Green
Brown
questions.explanation: Orange signs and markings identify temporary construction or maintenance work zones.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means what?
choices: Do not pass
Passing is allowed when safe
Two-way left-turn lane
Edge of the roadway
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because it is unsafe to cross.

questions.prompt: A downward-pointing triangular sign means what?
choices: Yield
Stop
Wrong way
No U-turn
questions.explanation: The inverted triangle is used only for yield signs, telling you to give the right of way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: What do white lines on a roadway separate?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
Traffic moving in opposite directions
The road from the sidewalk only
Parking spaces from driving lanes only
questions.explanation: White lines divide lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a steady yellow traffic light?
choices: Prepare to stop because the light is about to turn red
Speed up to beat the red light
Stop immediately no matter what
Treat it as a green light
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, who has the right of way when two vehicles arrive at the same time?
choices: The driver on the left yields to the driver on the right
The driver on the right yields to the driver on the left
The faster vehicle goes first
Whoever honks first
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an uncontrolled intersection together, the driver on the left must yield to the one on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, who must you yield to?
choices: Oncoming traffic and crossing pedestrians
No one, because you are turning
Only vehicles behind you
Only traffic to your left
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must wait for oncoming vehicles and pedestrians to clear before completing the turn.

questions.prompt: What must you do when a pedestrian is legally crossing in a crosswalk?
choices: Stop and yield the right of way to the pedestrian
Honk so they hurry up
Drive around them quickly
Proceed if you arrived first
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians lawfully crossing in a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When you are pulling out of a private driveway onto a road, who has the right of way?
choices: Traffic already on the road
You, because you are entering
Whoever is going faster
No one has the right of way
questions.explanation: A driver entering from a driveway, alley, or lot must yield to vehicles already traveling on the roadway.

questions.prompt: When you enter a roundabout, what must you do?
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout and enter on a safe gap
Stop in the circle and wait
Enter quickly before other cars
Always come to a full stop before entering
questions.explanation: Traffic already circulating in a roundabout has the right of way, so you enter only when there is a safe gap.

questions.prompt: On an undivided road, what must you do when a school bus ahead stops with red lights flashing and its stop arm out?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
Pass slowly on the left
Continue if no children are visible
Honk and drive around the bus
questions.explanation: Vehicles in both directions on an undivided road must stop for a school bus displaying flashing red lights and an extended stop arm.

questions.prompt: Who generally has the right of way when an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on?
choices: The emergency vehicle; you must pull over and stop
You, if you arrived first
Whichever vehicle is larger
Neither vehicle
questions.explanation: You must yield to an emergency vehicle using its lights and siren by pulling to the right and stopping until it passes.

questions.prompt: Before making a right turn, where should you position your vehicle?
choices: Close to the right edge of the lane
In the center of the road
As far left as possible
Straddling two lanes
questions.explanation: Moving close to the right edge before a right turn keeps you in the proper lane and prevents others from passing on your right.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, which vehicle should proceed first?
choices: The vehicle that arrived and stopped first
The largest vehicle
The vehicle turning left
The vehicle on the widest road
questions.explanation: At a four-way stop, the first vehicle to come to a complete stop has the right to go first.

questions.prompt: When should you signal before making a turn or lane change?
choices: Well in advance, before you begin the maneuver
Only after you start turning
Only if other cars are nearby
Signaling is optional
questions.explanation: Signaling early gives other drivers time to react to your intended turn or lane change.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the speed limit in a Maine business or residential district?
choices: 25 mph
30 mph
20 mph
35 mph
questions.explanation: Maine sets a default limit of 25 mph in business or residential districts unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit on other public ways (outside business or residential districts) in Maine?
choices: 45 mph
55 mph
35 mph
65 mph
questions.explanation: Where no other limit is posted, the default speed limit on Maine public ways is 45 mph.

questions.prompt: What does a posted speed limit represent?
choices: The maximum legal speed under ideal conditions
The minimum speed you must drive
A suggested cruising speed only
The speed to drive in any weather
questions.explanation: Posted limits are maximums for good conditions; you must slow down when conditions are poor.

questions.prompt: What is a recommended minimum following distance behind the vehicle ahead in good conditions?
choices: At least three to four seconds
One car length
Half a second
Whatever feels comfortable
questions.explanation: A three- to four-second gap gives you enough time to react and stop safely under normal conditions.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your following distance in rain, snow, or fog?
choices: Increase it to allow more time to stop
Decrease it to keep up with traffic
Keep it exactly the same
Follow closely to see better
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility mean you need a larger gap to stop safely in bad weather.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect the distance your vehicle needs to stop?
choices: The stopping distance increases
The stopping distance decreases
Stopping distance stays the same
Stopping distance becomes zero
questions.explanation: The faster you go, the longer it takes and the more distance you need to bring the vehicle to a stop.

questions.prompt: What should you do when another driver is tailgating you?
choices: Increase your following distance from the car ahead
Brake hard to warn them
Speed up well over the limit
Stop in the lane
questions.explanation: Leaving extra room in front gives you a cushion so you can brake gradually and avoid a chain collision.

questions.prompt: When must you reduce your speed below the posted limit?
choices: When weather, traffic, or road conditions make it unsafe
Only at night
Never, if the limit allows it
Only when police are present
questions.explanation: You must always drive at a speed that is safe for current conditions, even if that is below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than the flow of traffic can be dangerous because it can do what?
choices: Cause other drivers to brake suddenly or make risky passes
Improve traffic flow
Eliminate the need to signal
Increase your fuel economy and safety
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly disrupts traffic flow and can lead to unsafe passing and rear-end crashes.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, which lane should slower traffic generally use?
choices: The right lane
The left lane
The center lane only
Any lane they choose
questions.explanation: Slower vehicles should keep right so that faster traffic can pass safely on the left.

questions.prompt: When is it unsafe and usually illegal to pass another vehicle?
choices: On a hill or curve where you cannot see oncoming traffic
On a long straight road with a broken yellow line
When the road ahead is clearly visible
On a marked passing zone
questions.explanation: Passing where your view is blocked, such as on hills and curves, is dangerous and prohibited.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?
choices: When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
As soon as you pull alongside it
Immediately after your front bumper clears it
Whenever you feel like it
questions.explanation: Seeing the whole vehicle in your mirror means there is enough room to merge back without cutting it off.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, what should you always do in addition to signaling?
choices: Check mirrors and look over your shoulder for blind spots
Close your eyes and merge
Speed up to twice the limit
Honk and merge immediately
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show blind spots, so a shoulder check helps you spot vehicles you might otherwise miss.

questions.prompt: On a typical two-lane road, on which side do you normally pass another vehicle?
choices: The left
The right
Either side without checking
The shoulder
questions.explanation: Passing is normally done on the left where you have the clearest view of oncoming traffic.

questions.prompt: Is it legal to drive onto the shoulder to pass a vehicle?
choices: No, passing on the shoulder is not allowed
Yes, on any road
Yes, if you signal first
Yes, but only at night
questions.explanation: The shoulder is not a travel lane, and passing there is unsafe and illegal.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means what about passing?
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe to do so
Passing is never allowed
You must always pass here
The lanes go the same direction
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line on your side permits passing when the way ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: When passing on a two-lane road, when should you complete the pass?
choices: Well before any oncoming vehicle reaches you
Just as the oncoming vehicle arrives
After the oncoming vehicle passes you
It does not matter
questions.explanation: You must have plenty of room to finish passing and return to your lane before meeting oncoming traffic.

questions.prompt: What should you do when going through a turn or curve?
choices: Stay within your own lane
Drift into the next lane
Cross the center line to straighten it out
Brake hard in the middle of the curve
questions.explanation: Holding your lane through curves keeps you safely separated from traffic in adjacent lanes.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, what should drivers do to allow faster traffic to pass?
choices: Keep right except when passing
Stay in the left lane at all times
Weave between lanes
Drive in the center line
questions.explanation: Keeping right unless passing lets faster vehicles move by safely in the left lane.

questions.prompt: Under Maine's Move Over law, what must you do when approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with its lights flashing?
choices: Move over a lane if safe, or slow down if you cannot
Speed up to pass quickly
Maintain your speed in the same lane
Stop in the travel lane
questions.explanation: Maine's Move Over law requires changing lanes away from a stopped emergency or service vehicle, or slowing to a safe speed if you cannot move over.

questions.prompt: Maine's expanded Move Over law also applies to which of the following?
choices: Any disabled vehicle stopped with warning lights on
Only police cars
Only ambulances
Vehicles parked in driveways
questions.explanation: The law was expanded to include any disabled vehicle displaying warning lights, not just emergency vehicles.

questions.prompt: Why do motorcycles require extra attention from other drivers?
choices: They are smaller and harder to see, especially in blind spots
They always travel slower than cars
They cannot stop quickly
They are required to stay on the shoulder
questions.explanation: A motorcycle's small size makes it easy to overlook, so check carefully before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: How should you pass a bicyclist on the road?
choices: Slow down and give plenty of space before returning to your lane
Pass as close as possible to save time
Honk and squeeze by
Force the cyclist onto the shoulder
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are vulnerable, so reduce speed and leave a wide margin when passing them.

questions.prompt: What is true about the blind spots of large trucks?
choices: They are large, and if you cannot see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you
Trucks have no blind spots
Trucks can see all around them
Their blind spots are smaller than a car's
questions.explanation: Large trucks have big blind spots, so staying where you can see the mirrors keeps you visible to the driver.

questions.prompt: Compared with a car, a large truck needs what to stop and turn?
choices: More distance and space
Less distance and space
The same distance and space
No special consideration
questions.explanation: Because of their size and weight, trucks need much greater distances to stop and wider room to turn.

questions.prompt: What should you do when you encounter slow-moving farm equipment on a rural road?
choices: Slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal
Tailgate until it pulls over
Pass immediately on a curve
Honk continuously
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly, so be patient and pass only where you have a clear, legal opportunity.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian who is blind is crossing with a white cane or guide dog, what must you do?
choices: Stop and yield the right of way
Honk to alert them
Drive around them quickly
Proceed because you have the green light
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid lingering alongside a large truck?
choices: You may be in the truck's blind spot where the driver cannot see you
It is illegal to be near a truck
Trucks accelerate too quickly
It slows your fuel economy
questions.explanation: Staying beside a truck puts you in a blind spot, so pass through it promptly or stay back.

questions.prompt: In Maine, what is the blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for a driver age 21 or older?
choices: 0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.10 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: A driver 21 or older is considered legally impaired at a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Maine's zero-tolerance law, what BAC is allowed for drivers under 21?
choices: 0.00 percent (any measurable alcohol is illegal)
0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 may have no measurable alcohol; any amount can lead to license suspension.

questions.prompt: Under Maine's hands-free law, what is generally prohibited while driving?
choices: Holding or manipulating a handheld phone or device
Using a hands-free voice command
Talking to a passenger
Listening to the radio
questions.explanation: Maine's hands-free law bans holding or operating a handheld device; only hands-free use is allowed for adults.

questions.prompt: What are the phone rules for a driver under 18 or holding a learner's permit in Maine?
choices: They may not use any phone or device, even hands-free
They may text at red lights
They may use a handheld phone for calls
They have no phone restrictions
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 and permit holders are barred from using any phone or device, including hands-free, while driving.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It improves concentration
It sharpens vision
It has no real effect on driving
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions and clouds judgment, making safe driving impossible.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive?
choices: Some prescription and over-the-counter medications
Only illegal drugs
Only alcohol
Nothing but lack of sleep
questions.explanation: Many legal medications cause drowsiness or slowed reactions, so check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to handle texting while driving?
choices: Put the phone away and deal with messages after you stop
Text only at stop signs
Hold the phone low so police cannot see
Read messages while driving slowly
questions.explanation: Texting takes your eyes and mind off the road, so the safest choice is to wait until you are parked.

questions.prompt: What is the only thing that reliably reduces a person's BAC over time?
choices: Time
Coffee
A cold shower
Fresh air
questions.explanation: Only time lets the body process alcohol; coffee and other tricks do not lower BAC.

questions.prompt: Which of these counts as a driving distraction?
choices: Anything that takes your eyes, hands, or mind off driving
Only using a cell phone
Only eating
Only adjusting the radio
questions.explanation: Distraction is any activity that diverts your attention from the task of driving.

questions.prompt: If you must handle something that cannot wait while driving, what is the safest action?
choices: Pull over safely and stop first
Do it quickly while driving
Slow down and multitask
Ask a passenger to steer
questions.explanation: Pulling over removes the distraction from the act of driving and keeps everyone safer.

questions.prompt: In Maine, when must you turn on your headlights?
choices: When visibility is reduced and whenever your wipers are in continuous use
Only after midnight
Only on highways
Only when it is fully dark
questions.explanation: Maine law ties headlight use to reduced visibility and continuous wiper use so others can see you.

questions.prompt: What kind of headlights should you use when driving in fog?
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights only
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and worsen glare, so low beams give you better visibility.

questions.prompt: Why can the road be especially slippery during the first rain after a dry spell?
choices: Oil and grime rise to the surface and reduce traction
Water makes tires grip better
The road dries faster
Rain removes all hazards
questions.explanation: Built-up oil floats on the new water film, making the road slick until rain washes it away.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road typically freezes before the rest in cold weather?
choices: Bridges and overpasses
The center of straight roads
Uphill sections only
Well-traveled intersections
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from above and below, so they ice over sooner than ordinary roads.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, what should you do?
choices: Ease off the gas and steer gently in the direction you want to go
Slam on the brakes
Jerk the wheel sharply
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and steering smoothly helps the tires regain grip and keeps the vehicle under control.

questions.prompt: How should you drive on snow or ice?
choices: Slow down and make smooth, gentle steering and braking inputs
Drive normally
Brake hard at every turn
Accelerate quickly to keep momentum
questions.explanation: Gentle inputs and lower speed reduce the chance of skidding on slippery surfaces.

questions.prompt: If weather conditions become too dangerous to keep driving, what should you do?
choices: Pull completely off the road to a safe spot and use hazard lights
Stop in the travel lane
Speed up to get home faster
Turn off all your lights
questions.explanation: Getting fully off the road and using hazard lights keeps you and other drivers safe when visibility is very poor.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your speed in heavy rain or snow?
choices: Reduce your speed below the posted limit
Keep driving at the posted limit
Increase your speed
Maintain cruise control
questions.explanation: Lower speeds give you more control and stopping distance on wet or snowy roads.

questions.prompt: What should you do if bright sun or glare makes it hard to see ahead?
choices: Slow down, use your visor, and increase following distance
Speed up to get past it
Close your eyes briefly
Turn on your high beams
questions.explanation: Reducing speed and using your visor help you cope safely with reduced visibility from glare.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb lets the curb stop the car if it rolls forward.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels away from the curb means the car will roll back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: Where is it illegal to park?
choices: In front of a fire hydrant or blocking a crosswalk
In a marked, legal parking space
In your own driveway
In a metered space you paid for
questions.explanation: Parking that blocks hydrants, crosswalks, or intersections is prohibited because it creates hazards.

questions.prompt: Before leaving your parked vehicle, what should you do?
choices: Set the parking brake and shift to park or into gear
Leave it in neutral
Leave the engine running in gear
Leave the doors open
questions.explanation: Setting the brake and securing the transmission keeps the vehicle from rolling away.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into the street, what should you do?
choices: Check mirrors and over your shoulder for traffic and cyclists
Open it quickly to save time
Open it without looking
Honk first
questions.explanation: Checking for approaching vehicles and bicyclists prevents your door from causing a collision.

questions.prompt: Who may legally park in a space reserved for people with disabilities?
choices: Only a vehicle displaying a valid disability permit or plate
Anyone for a few minutes
Any vehicle when the lot is busy
Delivery vehicles only
questions.explanation: Accessible spaces are reserved for vehicles with a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: When pulling out of a parking space into traffic, what should you do?
choices: Signal and check carefully for approaching traffic before moving
Pull out quickly without looking
Rely only on your mirrors
Back out at full speed
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking for traffic helps you re-enter the roadway safely.

questions.prompt: What does a curb painted yellow generally indicate?
choices: A no-parking or restricted zone
Free unlimited parking
A loading zone for any vehicle
A bus-only lane
questions.explanation: Painted curbs mark parking restrictions, and yellow generally signals a no-parking or loading restriction.

questions.prompt: When you park parallel to the curb, how close should your tires be to it?
choices: Within about a foot of the curb
Several feet away in the travel lane
On top of the sidewalk
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Parking close to the curb keeps your vehicle out of the travel lane and out of traffic's way.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner's permit in Maine?
questions.explanation: Maine allows applications for a learner's permit beginning at age 15, with driver education required for those under 18.

questions.prompt: What must an applicant under 18 complete before applying for a Maine learner's permit?
choices: An approved driver education course
Two years of college
A defensive driving class only
Nothing in advance
questions.explanation: Maine requires applicants under 18 to finish an approved driver education course before getting a learner's permit.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Maine knowledge (written) exam, and what score is needed to pass?
choices: 30 questions, 80 percent to pass
20 questions, 70 percent to pass
40 questions, 75 percent to pass
25 questions, 90 percent to pass
questions.explanation: The Maine knowledge test has 30 questions and requires at least 80 percent correct to pass.

questions.prompt: How long must a driver under 18 hold a learner's permit before taking the road test in Maine?
choices: At least six months
At least one month
At least one year
There is no waiting period
questions.explanation: A driver under 18 must hold the permit for at least six months before the road test.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice must a Maine driver under 18 complete?
choices: 70 hours, including 10 hours of night driving
30 hours, including 5 at night
100 hours, including 20 at night
50 hours, all in daylight
questions.explanation: Maine requires 70 hours of supervised practice, of which at least 10 must be at night.

questions.prompt: During the first 270 days, when may a Maine intermediate license holder NOT drive?
choices: Between midnight and 5 a.m.
Between 8 p.m. and 6 a.m.
During morning rush hour
On weekends
questions.explanation: Intermediate license restrictions prohibit driving between midnight and 5 a.m. for the first 270 days.

questions.prompt: During the first 270 days of an intermediate license, who may ride as a passenger without a qualified supervisor present?
choices: Only immediate family members
Any friends
Up to three classmates
Anyone the driver chooses
questions.explanation: For the first 270 days, an intermediate license holder may carry only immediate family unless a qualified supervising driver is present.

questions.prompt: Who can serve as a supervising driver while a permit holder practices in Maine?
choices: A licensed driver at least 20 years old who has held a license for at least two years
Any licensed driver of any age
A driver who is at least 18
A friend with a learner's permit
questions.explanation: The supervising driver must be at least 20 and have held a license for two or more years.

questions.prompt: What score is required to pass the Maine road sign portion of the exam?
choices: 80 percent or higher
60 percent or higher
70 percent or higher
100 percent
questions.explanation: Like the knowledge test, the road sign test must be passed with a score of at least 80 percent.

questions.prompt: Under Maine's implied consent law, what happens if you refuse a chemical test after a lawful OUI arrest?
choices: Your license is automatically suspended
Nothing happens
You only pay a small fine
You get a warning
questions.explanation: By driving, you consent to testing, and refusing it triggers an automatic license suspension separate from OUI penalties.

questions.prompt: What can happen if a Maine driver violates the restrictions of their intermediate license?
choices: The restriction period is extended by another 270 days and a fine is added
Nothing, it is just a warning
They lose the license permanently
They must retake the written test only
questions.explanation: Violating intermediate license restrictions adds another 270 days of restrictions plus a fine.

questions.prompt: What documents should you be ready to show when a law enforcement officer requests them?
choices: License, registration, and proof of insurance
Only your license
Your birth certificate
Your vehicle's repair records
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry and present their license, registration, and proof of insurance on request.

questions.prompt: What does Maine law require regarding seat belts?
choices: The driver and passengers must wear seat belts
Only the driver must buckle up
Seat belts are optional for adults
Only front-seat occupants need them
questions.explanation: Maine law requires the driver and passengers to be properly restrained by seat belts.

questions.prompt: How must young children be secured in a vehicle in Maine?
choices: In an appropriate child safety seat or booster for their age, weight, and height
With a regular seat belt only
On an adult's lap
In the cargo area
questions.explanation: Children must ride in a child safety seat or booster suited to their age, weight, and height.

questions.prompt: Driving in Maine is best described as what?
choices: A privilege that can be suspended or revoked
An unconditional right
A guarantee for all adults
Something that cannot be taken away
questions.explanation: Driving is a privilege, and breaking the law can result in suspension or revocation of that privilege.

questions.prompt: What should you do if you are involved in a crash?
choices: Stop, check for injuries, and exchange information; report it if required
Drive away if no police are present
Move on if your car still runs
Wait a day before reporting
questions.explanation: The law requires you to stop, help anyone injured, exchange information, and report serious crashes.

questions.prompt: What is a likely consequence of being convicted of operating under the influence (OUI) in Maine?
choices: License suspension and other penalties
A simple verbal warning
No effect on your license
A discount on insurance
questions.explanation: An OUI conviction brings serious penalties, including license suspension, fines, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: What is the typical fine range for violating learner's permit or intermediate license restrictions in Maine?
choices: About $250 to $500
About $10 to $25
There is no fine
questions.explanation: Violations of these license restrictions generally carry a fine in the range of $250 to $500.
